item.title: E300 Econometric Methods
item.description: E300 Econometric Methods Zoom Revision Class Date and Time: April 22, 9-11 am BST Venue: Zoom Material: Chapter 36 “Large sample estimation and hypothesis testing” in the Handbook of Econometrics by Newey and McFadden(1994). Office Hours Thursdays, 17.00 - 18.00, starting this week, at the following locations: Oct. 17 - Supervision Room 1 Oct. 24 - Supervision Room 1 Oct. 31 - Supervision Room 1 Nov. 7 - Meade Room Nov.

Weiguang Liu Contact: 545 King’s College, University of Cambridge, UK, CB2 1ST Email: wl342@cam.ac.uk Webpage: Link Education Postdoctoral Research Fellow, UCL, 2023 - present Janeway Institute Visitor, University of Cambridge, 2023 - present Ph.D. in Economics, University of Cambridge, 2023 Visiting Assistant in Research, Yale University, Oct 2021 - Feb 2023 MPhil in Economic Research (with Distinction), University of Cambridge, 2016 - 2017 Bachelor of Science (with Distinction), Peking University, 2016 Major: Economics Minor: Mathematics and Applied Mathematics Research Working papers Normal approximation for U-statistics with cross-sectional dependence, PDF Nonparametric specification tests with cross-sectional dependence Git Repo High-dimensional covariance estimation with structural information, PDF, Git Repo Large Covariance Matrix Estimation with Auxiliary Text Information (with Shuyi Ge, Shaoran Li and Oliver Linton) Work in progress Nonlinear beta tests in asset returns (with Oliver Linton) Uniform law of large numbers and bootstrap for U-statistics with cross-sectional dependence Valid inference for regression models with many covariates and multi-way clustering (with Cheuk Ng) Prediction and conformal inference with interval observations of outcomes (with Aureo de Paula abd Elie Tamer) Experiences Teaching assistant University of Cambridge MPhil Course R301a Time Series, 2020-2021 MPhil Course E300 Econometric Methods, 2020-2021 MPhil Course E300 Econometric Methods (Best Teaching Award), 2019-2020 MPhil Course F400 Asset Pricing, 2018-2019 Undergraduate Paper 6 Banking and Finance, 2018-2019 MPhil Course E101 Applied Microeconomics, 2017-2018 Peking University Undergraduate Course Financial Markets, 2015-2016 Conferences and referee experiences European Winter Meeting of the Econometric Society, University of Manchester, Dec 17-19, 2023 Big data conference at Cambridge, May 2023 Econometric Society World Congress, 2020 Referee for the Journal of Econometrics Scholarships and prizes University of Cambridge Research Fund from the Faculty of Economics, University of Cambridge Grace & Thomas C H Chan Cambridge International Scholarship Peking University Excellent Graduate Awards by Beijing Municipal Commission of Education and Peking University Academic Excellence Award from 2013 to 2015, ACLI Scholarship, Robin Li Scholarship, May 4th Scholarship, Third Prize Freshman Scholarship 2013 Excellent Volunteer Teacher Skills Language: Chinese(Native), English(Fluent), Coding: Python, Matlab, R

item.title: F400 Asset Pricing
item.description: F400 Asset Pricing Office Hours Wednesday 5-6 pm 13th Feb, Supervision room 1; 20th Feb, Meade room; 27th Feb, Supervision room 2; 6th March, Supervision room 2; 13th March, Marshall room; 20th March, Supervision Room 1. Supplementary Materials Details on Computing the Certainty Equivalence in Chapter 2 Details on Black-Scholes Derivation Details on Finding the Term Structure Equation Solution to Vasicek Model

item.title: Paper 6 Banking and Finance
item.description: Paper 6 Banking and Finance About different channels Link to Bernanke speech Financial accelerator: changes in financial and credit conditions are important in the propagation of the business cycle, a mechanism that has been dubbed the “financial accelerator.” Credit channel: changes in financial conditions may amplify the effects of monetary policy on the economy, the so-called credit channel of monetarypolicy transmission. Credit channel works through (wiki link) Balance sheet channel Bank lending channel Models CSV model CE model Both models can show the financial acceleartor and the credit channel: roughly speaking, the models predict/explain that when a shock happens, it will be amplified through the banking system because banks will change credit rationing behaviour.

item.title: Research
item.description: Research Working papers Normal approximation for U-statistics with cross-sectional dependence PDF Nonparametric specification tests with cross-sectional dependence Git Repo High-dimensional covariance estimation with structural information PDF, Git Repo Large Covariance Matrix Estimation with Auxiliary Text Information (with Shuyi Ge, Shaoran Li and Oliver Linton) Prediction and conformal inference with interval observations of outcomes (with Aureo de Paula abd Elie Tamer) Work in progress Nonlinear beta tests in asset returns (with Oliver Linton) Uniform law of large numbers and bootstrap for U-statistics with cross-sectional dependence Valid inference for regression models with many covariates and multi-way clustering (with Cheuk Ng)

item.title: Teaching
item.description: Teaching 2021 R301 Time Series E300 Econometric Methods 2020 E300 Econometric Methods 2019 F400 Asset Pricing Paper 6 Banking and Finance 2018 E101 Applied Microeconomics
